name: Margaret Holloway
chief_complaint: Poorly controlled blood glucose and worsening fatigue

visit_history.notes: HbA1c worsened from 8.4% to 9.1%. Patient reports dietary non-compliance over holidays. BP still above target. Discussed adding SGLT2 inhibitor; deferred due to UTI history.
HbA1c 8.4%. Glipizide dose increased. Counseled on carbohydrate counting. BP 142/88 — lisinopril dose increased from 5 mg to 10 mg.
Routine annual visit. HbA1c 7.9%, patient was adherent. No new complaints. Discussed weight loss and referral to dietitian accepted.

name: Robert Castillo
chief_complaint: Shortness of breath on exertion and ankle swelling
active_conditions: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF, EF 30%)

visit_history.notes: Patient reports 2-pillow orthopnea and 2+ pitting edema bilaterally. BNP elevated at 820. Furosemide dose increased. Repeat echo scheduled — last EF was 30%.
Stable HF symptoms, BNP was 540. Empagliflozin added for HF and CKD benefit. Potassium trending up to 4.8 on spironolactone — monitoring closely.
Admitted for acute decompensated HF. IV diuresis with 80 mg furosemide. Discharged on Entresto after ACE inhibitor-to-ARNi switch. AF rate controlled.

name: Dorothy Kim
chief_complaint: Elevated creatinine on routine labs, fatigue

visit_history.notes: eGFR declined from 44 to 38 over 6 months. Phosphorus elevated at 5.2 — sevelamer considered instead of calcium-based binder. Potassium 5.1, counseled on low-K diet. Nephrology referral for transplant evaluation initiated.
Anemia stable on darbepoetin. Metformin discontinued 3 months ago due to eGFR drop below 45. Switched to insulin. HbA1c improved to 7.8%.
eGFR 44. Discussed CKD progression risk factors. Blood pressure still above target 130/80 — losartan dose up-titrated.

name: James Whitfield
chief_complaint: Palpitations and INR out of therapeutic range

visit_history.notes: INR subtherapeutic at 1.8. Patient reports started amoxicillin from dentist 2 weeks ago. Warfarin dose held at 5 mg, recheck in 1 week. Discussed DOAC switch given variable INR control — patient reluctant.
AF paroxysmal, 2 episodes logged on Kardia monitor. CHA₂DS₂-VASc score 4 (age, HTN, TIA, sex). Continuing anticoagulation. Discussed catheter ablation — patient interested.
INR therapeutic at 2.4. No bleeding events. BP controlled. Metoprolol dose adequate for rate control.

name: Patricia Nguyen
chief_complaint: Weight gain and worsening glycemic control; considering GLP-1 therapy

visit_history.notes: Referred for GLP-1 receptor agonist initiation. HbA1c 8.6% despite dual oral therapy. Weight increased 8 lbs since last visit. Liver enzymes elevated consistent with NAFLD. Semaglutide considered for dual benefit on weight and glucose.
DPP-4 inhibitor added at last visit. Minimal HbA1c improvement. Patient motivated for weight loss. Discussed bariatric surgery vs pharmacotherapy.
HbA1c 9.0%. Metformin dose maximized. Counseled on lifestyle. Patient declined insulin. Sitagliptin added.

BP refractory despite 3 agents at max doses. Aldosterone-renin ratio elevated suggesting primary aldosteronism. Adrenal CT ordered. Hypokalemia noted (3.3) — potassium supplementation started. Spironolactone being considered.
Sleep study confirmed moderate OSA, AHI 22. CPAP initiated. BP did not improve significantly — secondary causes workup initiated.
BP 158/94. Chlorthalidone added as third agent. White coat effect ruled out by home monitoring (average 161/95). Ambulatory BP monitoring performed.

name: Eleanor Voss
chief_complaint: Worsening dyspnea, COPD exacerbation vs. heart failure decompensation
active_conditions: Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF, EF 55%)

visit_history.notes: Presented with 3 days of worsening dyspnea and productive cough. Exam shows bilateral crackles and wheezing. BNP 410, SpO2 91%. Difficult to distinguish COPD exacerbation from HFpEF decompensation. Started prednisone + azithromycin for COPD, IV furosemide 40 mg for HF. Improved with combined treatment.
Spirometry confirmed GOLD III COPD. Beta-blockers contraindicated. Diltiazem used for AFib rate control instead. Pulmonary rehab referral placed.
Echo showed EF 55%, grade II diastolic dysfunction. Diagnosed HFpEF. Diuresis optimized. AFib rate-controlled, rhythm control not pursued given age and COPD.

name: Marcus Chen
chief_complaint: Hyperkalemia found on routine labs, CKD-related medication adjustment needed

visit_history.notes: Potassium 5.8 on routine labs — called back urgently. ECG normal. Spironolactone held. Dietary counseling provided. Patiromer considered to allow ACE inhibitor continuation for renal protection. Low bicarbonate consistent with metabolic acidosis from CKD.
eGFR stable at 38 for past 6 months. Spironolactone added for additional BP control and proteinuria reduction. Potassium was 5.2 — monitoring more frequently now.
Metformin stopped as eGFR dropped below 45. Insulin regimen optimized. eGFR 41. Discussed CKD diet restrictions.

name: Sandra Okafor
chief_complaint: Anticoagulation decision in AFib with advanced CKD

CHA2DS2-VASc score.unit: (HTN, age>75, DM, female, prior stroke risk)

visit_history.notes: Discussion of anticoagulation risks vs benefits. CHA₂DS₂-VASc 7 indicates high stroke risk. History of GI bleed in 2024 (upper GI, treated endoscopically). eGFR 22 — DOACs partially renally cleared and require dose adjustment or avoidance. Continuing warfarin with careful INR monitoring. HAS-BLED score 4.
eGFR declined from 28 to 22 over 6 months. Preparing for dialysis — AV fistula creation being considered. Dialysis education started.
GI bleed occurred August 2025 — upper endoscopy revealed peptic ulcer, H. pylori treated. Anticoagulation held for 4 weeks, resumed. Pantoprazole continued indefinitely.

name: Frank Petersen
chief_complaint: Fluid management in HFpEF with AFib and CKD — triple comorbidity
active_conditions: Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction (HFpEF, EF 60%)

visit_history.notes: Empagliflozin added per EMPEROR-Preserved trial evidence. Volume status improved with torsemide switch from furosemide (better bioavailability). BNP down from 480 to 290. eGFR stable. AFib rate-controlled.
BNP 480 with symptomatic fluid retention. Diuretic changed from furosemide to torsemide. Candesartan added for BP and HFpEF. Discussed weight monitoring daily.
Echo: EF 60%, grade II/III diastolic dysfunction, LA enlargement. Persistent AFib confirmed. CKD unchanged eGFR 50. BMI 36 — weight management program referral.

name: Angela Torres
chief_complaint: Leg pain with walking, peripheral artery disease management

visit_history.notes: Claudication limiting walking to 1.5 blocks. ABI stable. Cilostazol continued. Vascular anatomy mapped — angioplasty considered if conservative management fails. Wound check on right great toe (minor abrasion) — healing well. Daily foot inspection reinforced.
COMPASS trial data reviewed — rivaroxaban 2.5 mg BID + aspirin discussed for MACE reduction in PAD. Patient hesitant due to bleeding concerns. Continuing dual antiplatelet for now.
PAD diagnosed by ABI testing. Statin intensified to atorvastatin 80 mg. Supervised exercise program initiated. Smoking cessation confirmed 3 years — encouraged.

name: Ryan Nakamura
chief_complaint: Type 1 diabetes with recurrent hypoglycemia and thyroid disorder

visit_history.notes: Time below range at 5.2% — above recommended <4%. Hypoglycemia unawareness confirmed on structured questionnaire. Sensor glucose alarm threshold raised. Closed-loop algorithm settings adjusted. TSH 4.8 — levothyroxine dose increase from 88 mcg to 100 mcg considered.
Switched from MDI to Omnipod 5 closed-loop system 4 months ago. HbA1c improved from 7.6% to 6.9%. TIR improving but nocturnal hypoglycemia episodes persist — basal rate adjusted.
HbA1c 7.6% on MDI. Hashimoto's diagnosed — levothyroxine started. Patient concerned about hypoglycemia while exercising — discussed carbohydrate supplementation strategies.

name: Brenda Harrington
chief_complaint: Metabolic syndrome workup; elevated triglycerides and pre-hypertension

visit_history.notes: Triglycerides remain very elevated at 398 despite fenofibrate. Icosapent ethyl (Vascepa) being considered per REDUCE-IT trial evidence. Statin allergy limits LDL management — PCSK9 inhibitor discussed. Lifestyle intensification counseled. HbA1c 6.0 — screening for T2DM annually.
Fenofibrate started — triglycerides down from 512 to 398. Patient reports mild muscle aches on prior statins. Bempedoic acid discussed as statin-free LDL lowering option.
Diagnosed with metabolic syndrome meeting 4/5 criteria. Intensive lifestyle counseling and Mediterranean diet referral. Weight loss goal set at 7% body weight.

name: Harold Jenkins
chief_complaint: Approaching dialysis — CKD Stage 5, access planning

visit_history.notes: eGFR 12 — approaching initiation threshold. AV fistula placed in left forearm 3 months ago, maturing well (thrill palpable). Discussed hemodialysis vs peritoneal dialysis options. PD preferred by patient. Hyperphosphatemia poorly controlled — dietitian review. PTH 380 — cinacalcet being considered.

eGFR declined from 18 to 12 over 4 months. Dialysis access surgery (AV fistula left forearm) performed. Sodium bicarbonate dose increased. Anemia partially responsive to ESA — ferritin checked, iron replete.
eGFR 18. Dialysis education completed. Patient and family attended CKD class. Transplant evaluated — not a candidate due to age and cardiac risk. AV fistula planning initiated.

name: Gloria Martinez
chief_complaint: Post-MI secondary prevention, dual antiplatelet therapy duration decision
active_conditions: Recent STEMI (6 months ago, LAD territory)

visit_history.notes: 6 months post-STEMI with drug-eluting stent to LAD. EF improved from 30% to 40% on GDMT. Ticagrelor 90 mg BID — discussing transition to 60 mg BID at 12 months per PEGASUS data. Clopidogrel avoided due to CYP2C19 poor metabolizer status. Eplerenone added for EF <40% post-MI. LDL well-controlled at 48.

Echo shows EF improving to 36% from 30%. GDMT optimized — ramipril up-titrated, metoprolol continued, eplerenone added. Empagliflozin started for HF benefit per EMPEROR data. Patient tolerating all medications well. No angina.

Discharged 1 week post-primary PCI to LAD. EF 30% at discharge. Started on GDMT. CYP2C19 genotyping resulted — poor metabolizer, ticagrelor chosen over clopidogrel. Cardiac rehab enrollment confirmed.
